records.ety: [AS. nǣfre; ne not, no + ǣfre ever.]

defs: Not ever; not at any time; at no time, whether past, present, or future.
quotes: Death still draws nearer, never seeming near.

defs: In no degree; not in the least; not.
quotes: Whosoever has a friend to guide him, may carry his eyes in another man's head, and yet see never the worse.
And he answered him to never a word.
Ask me never so much dower and gift.
A fear of battery, . . . though never so well grounded, is no duress.
notes: ☞ Never is much used in composition with present participles to form adjectives, as in never-ceasing, never-dying, never-ending, never-fading, never-failing, etc., retaining its usual signification.
